what_it_does: This file is a structured design-substrate extraction of the ConceptV / Project Vi deck1-2026 pitch deck. It documents the deck's narrative arc, a slide-by-slide attention flow, and a catalog of visual-system findings (chrome modes, color, geometry, typography, spacing, atoms, zones, and layout grammar) with recipes, evidence, and generator rules.
observations: The file contains a 16-slide deck analysis split into narrative_arc, attention_flow, and findings.
Two chrome modes are defined: immersive full-bleed 3D architectural renders with UI chrome (p-01, p-16) and warm near-white content slides with a faint blueprint watermark (p-02 to p-15).
attention_flow gives an explicit eye-path description for every slide.
findings are categorized into chrome-mode, gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, line-connector, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, zone, and sequence.
Each finding includes a recipe, evidence slide list, emergent boolean, and generator_rule.
A strict mono-accent gold palette (#C9A227) is used as the only color accent against warm cream backgrounds.
Geometry is semantically assigned: hexagon = Property Wizard hub, diamond = Vi product, circle = person/hub/integration, rounded rectangle = pills/containers/highlights.
Line grammar distinguishes solid arrows (transformation) from dashed connectors (integration/planned/future).
Type hierarchy includes content-budget rules (character counts, line limits) and an 8px modular spacing rhythm.
A persistent golden V badge sits in the bottom-right corner of every content slide.
Left-visual / right-content splits are used for argument slides; full-width centered layouts are used for proof/validation slides.
standouts: The attention_flow array maps explicit eye-entry and scan sequences for each slide, treating layout as choreographed reading.
findings encode generative rules with emergent flags, making the deck reproducible as a visual grammar.
The immersive chrome mode is restricted to opening and closing bookends only.
The bullet marker is a custom golden triangle arrow, not a standard glyph.
The 8px modular grid and strict single-line bullet/pill constraints are specified as hard generator rules.
conventions: Follows a JSON schema with collection, depth_level, what_it_is, narrative_arc, attention_flow, and findings.
Each finding uses category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, and generator_rule fields.
Uses approximate hex values, pixel dimensions, and deck-scale measurements.
Maintains a neutral, descriptive tone without evaluative ranking.
Categorizes design tokens by function (geometry, line-connector, atom, zone, etc.).
concerns: The file is truncated mid-section in the sequence finding.
Attention-flow claims are subjective and not validated by eye-tracking data.
Color and sizing values are approximate and may not match source assets exactly.
No source image URLs, asset references, or original deck dimensions are provided.
The emergent boolean lacks explicit criteria for how a pattern is judged emergent versus designed.
The specification is tightly tied to one deck and may not generalize without additional abstraction.
notes: A granular, rule-based design autopsy of the ConceptV 2026 pitch deck that treats each slide as a reproducible visual-grammar instance.
questions: What is the exact source resolution and aspect ratio of the original deck?
How were the attention-flow sequences validated?
What criteria distinguish emergent findings from non-emergent ones?
What does the truncated sequence section contain?
Are the listed hex colors measured from source files or estimated visually?
How should the system scale these rules to other formats or responsive outputs?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a ledger of code/design substrates; actual content is a deck-specific design-system extraction. It fits the substrate role but is more visual-design specification than code.
apparent_themes: Visual grammar as a generative system
Mono-accent premium warmth derived from architectural interiors
Consistency through repeated tokens and layout grammars
Bookend experiential proof bracketing rational argument
Rule-based design tokens with explicit generator constraints
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make the deck fully reproducible by encoding every visual decision into explicit recipes and constraints, and to separate emotional/experiential slides from argumentative content slides so the system can generate consistent ConceptV collateral.
novelty: It treats a pitch deck as a formal grammar with eye-flow choreography, emergent-vs-core rule classification, and explicit generator constraints rather than as a static set of screenshots.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, reusable design-language specification and a per-slide attention map that a fused system could keep as a reference for generating or auditing consistent ConceptV deck collateral.
summary_for_embedding: A structured extraction of the ConceptV deck1-2026 16-slide investor pitch deck, cataloguing its narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and a generative design system including chrome modes, color palette, geometry, typography, spacing, atoms, zones, and layout grammar.
intention_for_embedding: To encode the deck's visual and narrative logic into reproducible design-substrate rules for consistent regeneration and extension.
